text: Indeed, We sent Noah to his people ňĻsaying to himňļ, ‚ÄúWarn your people before a painful punishment comes to them.‚ÄĚ
Noah proclaimed, ‚ÄúO my people! I am truly sent to you with a clear warning:
worship Allah ňĻaloneňļ, fear Him, and obey me.
He will forgive your sins, and delay your end until the appointed time. <sup foot_note=1238>1238</sup> Indeed, when the time set by Allah comes, it cannot be delayed, if only you knew!‚ÄĚ

foot_notes.1238: Meaning, it is out of Allah‚Äôs mercy that He does not hasten your punishment but allows you more time to repent.

text: He cried, ‚ÄúMy Lord! I have surely called my people day and night,
but my calls only made them run farther away.
And whenever I invite them to be forgiven by You, they press their fingers into their ears, cover themselves with their clothes, persist ňĻin denialňļ, and act very arrogantly.
Then I certainly called them openly,
then I surely preached to them publicly and privately,
saying, ‚ÄėSeek your Lord‚Äôs forgiveness, ňĻforňļ He is truly Most Forgiving.
He will shower you with abundant rain,
supply you with wealth and children, and give you gardens as well as rivers.
What is the matter with you that you are not in awe of the Majesty of Allah,
when He truly created you in stages ňĻof developmentňļ? <sup foot_note=1239>1239</sup>
Do you not see how Allah created seven heavens, one above the other,
placing the moon within them as a ňĻreflectedňļ light, and the sun as a ňĻradiantňļ lamp?
Allah ňĻaloneňļ caused you <sup foot_note=1240>1240</sup> to grow from the earth like a plant.

foot_notes.1240: i.e., your father Adam, who was created from clay.

text: Then He will return you to it, and then simply bring you forth ňĻagainňļ.
And Allah ňĻaloneňļ spread out the earth for you
to walk along its spacious pathways.‚Äô‚ÄĚ
ňĻEventually,ňļ Noah cried, ‚ÄúMy Lord! They have certainly persisted in disobeying me, and followed ňĻinsteadňļ those ňĻeliteňļ whose ňĻabundantňļ wealth and children only increase them in loss,
and who have devised a tremendous plot,
urging ňĻtheir followersňļ, ‚ÄėDo not abandon your idols‚ÄĒespecially Wadd , Suw√Ę‚Äô , Yagh√Ľth , Ya‚Äô√Ľq , and Nasr .‚Äô <sup foot_note=1241>1241</sup>

foot_notes.1241: These are the names of their main idols. Initially, these idols had been erected to commemorate some righteous people before the time of Noah. After many generations, however, the idols were taken as objects of worship.

text: Those ňĻeliteňļ have already led many astray. So ňĻO Lordňļ, only allow the wrongdoers to stray farther away.‚ÄĚ
So because of their sins, they were drowned, then admitted into the Fire. And they found none to help them against Allah.
Noah had prayed, ‚ÄúMy Lord! Do not leave a single disbeliever on earth.
For if You spare ňĻany ofňļ them, they will certainly mislead Your servants, and give birth only to ňĻwickedňļ sinners, staunch disbelievers.
My Lord! Forgive me, my parents, and whoever enters my house in faith, and ňĻallňļ believing men and women. And increase the wrongdoers only in destruction.‚ÄĚ
